evals.prompt: I want to add a new skill to this library for designing caching strategies (cache invalidation, TTLs, cache layers). Help me write it.
evals.expected_output: Should follow the authoring loop: clarify the trigger scenarios, draft a WHEN-focused description in the repo's `<purpose>. Triggers: ... . <boundary>` format under ~350 chars, keep SKILL.md tight with deep detail in references/, and produce exactly 3 evals. Should register it in skill-router.
assertions: Writes a description focused on when to use it (with a Triggers keyword list), not the internal workflow
Uses progressive disclosure (tight SKILL.md, references/ for deep detail)
Produces exactly 3 evals (happy / edge / scope boundary) with verifiable assertions
Mentions registering the skill in skill-router
Does not over-stuff SKILL.md with general knowledge Claude already has

evals.prompt: My `data-modeling` skill never seems to trigger even when people ask about schema design. How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose this as a description/triggering problem first (SDO + listing budget), not rewrite the workflow body. Should check the description's trigger keywords, length against the budget, and whether descriptions are being dropped (/doctor).
assertions: Treats it as a description/triggering issue, not a body-content issue
Inspects the trigger keywords and suggests widening them to match user phrasing
Checks the description length against the ~350-char listing budget
Mentions /doctor / the listing budget as a possible cause of dropped descriptions
Does not immediately rewrite the workflow steps

evals.prompt: Can you write the API documentation for our payments service?
evals.expected_output: This is a request to write docs for a project, not to author a skill. Should not invoke the skill-writing workflow; should hand off to project-documentation (or api-design).
assertions: Recognizes this is not about authoring a library skill
Does not apply the skill-writing loop
Hands off to project-documentation (and/or api-design) as the right skill
